betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Antimatter Containment: Neutral Exotic Containment Chamber§f

§aThe Neutral Exotic Containment chamber holds antimatter and exotic particles in a stable trap.§f
The §bExotic Containment Chamber Controller§f (`qmd:neutral_containment_controller`) is the brain of a §bNeutral Exotic Containment§f multiblock, built on a §bVacuum Chamber§f hull. It is the §bantimatter safe§f of QMD: use it to store antimatter (anti-hydrogen, anti-deuterium, etc.) safely, or to synthesize antimatter from antiproton + positron inputs.

§6Controller Crafting Recipe§f:
Per `QMDCraftingRecipeHandler.java:219`:
- Output: §bExotic Containment Controller§f (`QMDBlocks.exoticContainmentController`)
- Pattern (3×3): §bP-E-P / B-F-B / P-E-P§f
  - §bP§f = §bElite Plate§f
  - §bE§f = §bExtreme Ingot§f
  - §bB§f = §bElite Processor§f
  - §bF§f = §bVacuum Chamber Casing§f

§6Hull Structure (VacuumChamberLogic.java:35)§f:
The controller sits on top of a §bVacuum Chamber§f hull:
- §bInterior§f: 5×5×5 to 7×7×7 (so exterior 7×7×7 to 9×9×9)
- §bHull§f: built from §bVacuum Chamber Casing§f and §bVacuum Chamber Glass§f
- §bInternal Coils§f: §bVacuum Chamber Coils§f (BSCCO Wire + Osmiridium) form the magnetic confinement field
- §bBeam Ports§f: §bVacuum Chamber Beam Ports§f for antiparticle input/output
- §bEnergy Ports§f: §bVacuum Chamber Energy Ports§f (BSCCO Wire + Osmiridium) for power
- §bCoolant Vents§f: §bVacuum Chamber Vents§f (Servo + Osmiridium) for cooling

§6Antimatter Storage§f:
- Anti-hydrogen, anti-deuterium, anti-tritium, anti-helium-3, and anti-helium cells can be §bloaded into the chamber§f for safe storage.
- The chamber maintains a §bstrong magnetic trap§f via the §bBSCCO coils§f and §bOsmiridium hull§f, keeping antimatter from contacting the chamber walls.

§6Antimatter Recipes (AcceleratorSourceRecipes.java:35-39)§f:
Per the §bAccelerator Ion Source§f, an Empty Cell can be §bfilled with antimatter§f by feeding antiprotons or antideuterons into the source with the cell placed inside:
- Empty Cell + §bantiproton particle§f → §bAnti-Hydrogen Cell§f (`qmd:cell:1`)
- Empty Cell + §bantideuteron particle§f → §bAnti-Deuterium Cell§f (`qmd:cell:2`)
- Empty Cell + §bantitriton particle§f → §bAnti-Tritium Cell§f (`qmd:cell:3`)
- Empty Cell + §bantihelion particle§f → §bAnti-Helium-3 Cell§f (`qmd:cell:4`)
- Empty Cell + §bantialpha particle§f → §bAnti-Helium Cell§f (`qmd:cell:5`)

§6Containment Safety§f:
- Power loss → §bantimatter detonation§f: §cradiation burst§f, potential base vaporization
- Temperature > §bMAX_TEMP§f → containment field collapse
- Block break while storing antimatter → §cinstant detonation§f

§cWarning§f: Always wire the chamber to a §bbackup battery bank§f (e.g. §bRfTools Powercell§f, §bEnder IO Capacitor Bank§f) and a §bfail-safe§f to dump antimatter cells to a §bsafe storage§f if the chamber overheats.

§eTip§f: §bAntiproton particles§f are produced in a §bCollision Chamber§f by colliding high-energy proton beams. To bootstrap antimatter production, build a §bSynchrotron§f that accelerates protons to GeV energies, then route the output into a §bCollision Chamber§f facing an antiproton-yielding recipe.
